title: Horkelia kingii
intro: Horkelia kingii, also known as King's Horkelia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of King's Horkelia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other medicinal plants to create remedies for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Horkelia kingii is classified in the family Rosaceae, which includes other flowering plants such as roses and apples. The genus Horkelia is a group of low-growing, flowering plants native to North America. Horkelia kingii, specifically, is a species of flowering plant known for its pink or white blooms. It is typically found in mountainous regions and is considered an alpine plant. The classification of Horkelia kingii is further divided into the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida.
